title: DCET Admit Card 2026 OUT

detail.title: DCET Admit Card 2026 OUT
detail.short_info: DCET Admit Card 2026 has been released online at cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea. Download your Karnataka Diploma CET hall ticket using User ID and password, and check exam date 23 May 2026, exam time, test centre address and important instructions here.

content.text: The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) has released the DCET Admit Card 2026 for the Karnataka Diploma Common Entrance Test on the official KEA portal. Candidates who submitted the DCET 2026 application form can now log in and download their hall ticket in online mode only.

As per the official schedule, the Karnataka DCET 2026 exam will be conducted on 23 May 2026 across designated centres in the state, for lateral entry admission to 2nd year B.E/B.Tech and 1st year B.Arch courses for diploma holders.
Direct Link to Download DCET Admit Card 2026 Here

links.text: Direct Link to Download DCET Admit Card 2026 Here

rows: Lateral entry to 2nd year B.E/B.Tech and admission to B.Arch for eligible diploma holders
DCET Admit Card 2026 released online on the KEA DCET‑2026 portal
DCET 2026 User ID/Application number, password and captcha/security code
Candidate name, photo, hall ticket number, course applied, exam date & time, exam centre name & address, and exam‑day instructions
Documents to carry on exam day
Printed DCET Admit Card 2026 and one original valid photo ID (Aadhaar, PAN, Passport, Voter ID, Driving Licence, etc.)

sections.heading: How to Download DCET Admit Card 2026

items: Visit the KEA official site: https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea and click on the “DCET‑2026” link, or open the login page https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/dcet2026app/login directly.
On the DCET‑2026 page, click on “DCET‑2026 Admit Card Download Link”.
Enter your User ID / Application Number and Password, then type the captcha/security code shown on the screen.
Click on “Login / Submit” to access your DCET 2026 candidate dashboard.
Click on the “Download Admit Card / Hall Ticket” button; your DCET Admit Card 2026 will be displayed as a PDF.
Verify all details (name, paper, exam date, centre, shift) and download and print 2-3 clear A4‑size copies of the hall ticket.

content.text: If you forget your User ID or password, use the “Forgot User ID / Password” option on the login page (if available) or check your registration email/SMS for the login details.

sections.heading: Details Mentioned on DCET Admit Card 2026

content.text: After downloading, candidates must cross‑check the following details:

items: Candidate’s name and photograph
DCET 2026 User ID/application number and hall ticket number
Exam name: Diploma CET (DCET) 2026
Course applied for (B.E/B.Tech lateral entry / B.Arch, etc.)
Exam date: 23 May 2026, and exam time/duration as per the paper timetable
Exam centre name, code and full address
Candidate’s category and other application details (if printed)
Important exam‑day instructions and reporting/gate‑closing time

content.text: If there is any discrepancy in the admit card (name, photograph, course, or exam centre), candidates should immediately contact KEA through the official helpdesk before the exam date.

sections.heading: Documents Required with DCET Admit Card 2026

content.text: On the day of the Karnataka DCET 2026 exam, candidates must carry:

items: A printed copy of the DCET Admit Card 2026 (clear A4‑size printout; colour or good‑quality black and white).
One original valid photo ID proof, such as: Aadhaar Card Pan Card Passport Voter ID Driving Licence Any other government‑issued ID accepted by KEA.
Aadhaar Card
Pan Card
Passport
Voter ID
Driving Licence
Any other government‑issued ID accepted by KEA.
Additional documents if specified (e.g., PwD certificate and scribe permission letter for candidates availing scribe facility).

content.text: Entry to the exam hall will not be permitted without the printed admit card and valid photo ID.
